signature_dishes: 葱烧鱼头 (scallion oil fish head)
蟹粉砂锅饭 (crabmeat & roe sizzling rice)
vibe_and_space: Tiny, packed, walls covered in autographed photos. Two cramped floors, tables 30cm apart, lots of shouting. No-frills service.
one_paragraph_recommendation: The most internationally famous old-school Shanghainese in the city, holding cult status since the early 2000s. Reservations are notoriously hard — call weeks ahead and persist. Recommend specifically when a visitor wants 'real Shanghai food in a real Shanghai room' — the cramped chaos is the point. Not for a date where atmosphere matters, not for vegetarians, not for the noise-averse. The braised pork belly and crab fried rice are genuinely outstanding; the rest is solidly traditional.
contradictions: Editorial reviews unanimously praise the food but warn explicitly about the cramped space and brusque service — some visitors love this, some hate it. Mismatch with 'special occasion' framing.
